title: Sexual and reproductive health fund: Climate, nature and economy lens public statement - Canada.ca
content:
## Sexual and reproductive health fund: Climate, nature and economy lens public statement
Health Canada, Health Policy Branch January 30, 2025
- Environmental analysis
- Strategic economic analysis
- Cross-cutting considerations
In line with the Cabinet Directive on Strategic Environmental and Economic Assessment (SEEA), a strategic environmental and economic analysis was conducted on the proposal to expand and make permanent the Sexual and Reproductive Health Fund. The fund provides funding to community-based organizations that help improve access to sexual and reproductive health care services and supports for Canadians. This funding will support Canadians from marginalized and underserved communities to better get the sexual and reproductive health care they need, including abortion services.
The proposal also includes funding to Statistics Canada, starting in 2025-26, for two new surveys on sexual and reproductive health and rights.

## Environmental analysis
The environmental analysis did not result in findings of any significant environmental effects. As such, no strategic environmental analysis was done.

## Strategic economic analysis
A strategic economic analysis was conducted and found no local or national impacts, with sectoral low short term economic impacts and no long-term impacts.

## Cross-cutting considerations

### Federal Sustainable Development Strategy
The proposal supports Goal 5 of the 2022-2026 Federal Sustainable Development Strategy and Implementation Strategy "Reduce systemic barriers to gender equality." It also indirectly supports Sustainable Development Goals Global Indicator Framework target 5.6.
Indigenous Peoples were identified as one group among marginalized and underserved communities who face barriers to accessing sexual and reproductive health care. Indigenous organizations will be among the eligible recipients for funding from the Sexual and Reproductive Health Fund.
Engagement with external and internal stakeholders have informed elements of the proposal. No environmental issues were raised.
